title: PagerDuty Stakeholder Subscription Noise Ratio on Sev-1 Incidents
dataSources: PagerDuty incidents with stakeholder subscriber lists (`subscriber_emails` MV field)
app: PagerDuty REST include[]=subscribers or custom workflow enrichment
spl:
index=alerts sourcetype="pagerduty:incident"
| where urgency="high" OR severity="critical"
| eval sub_count=mvcount(subscriber_emails)
| where sub_count>25
| stats avg(sub_count) as avg_sub by service_name
| sort -avg_sub
description: Over-subscribed SEV-1 incidents flood executives and slow consensus.
value: Guides comms hygiene and subscription policy updates.
implementation: Truncate emails at ingest for privacy; use hashed IDs in shared indexes.
knownFalsePositives: Noisy updates during major customer incidents, executive bridges, or after notification settings change; that can be expected during a sev-1.
grandmaExplanation: We see how many extra people get the big incident mail compared with who must act, so sev-1 comms are clear and not a reply-all storm.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure: PagerDuty REST include[]=subscribers or custom workflow enrichment.
• Data: PagerDuty incidents with stakeholder subscriber lists (`subscriber_emails` MV field).
• For directory layout and inputs, see docs/implementation-guide.md.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Truncate emails at ingest for privacy; use hashed IDs in shared indexes.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following search (then save as report or alert; tune time range and threshold):

```spl
index=alerts sourcetype="pagerduty:incident"
| where urgency="high" OR severity="critical"
| eval sub_count=mvcount(subscriber_emails)
| where sub_count>25
| stats avg(sub_count) as avg_sub by service_name
| sort -avg_sub
```

Understanding this search
Over-subscribed SEV-1 incidents flood executives and slow consensus.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the PagerDuty incident timeline and service view for the same window. Reconcile a few incident numbers, state changes, and acknowledgement times. If anything disagrees, check export lag, time zone, and which API fields we map.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add to a dashboard, wire alert actions, and document ownership. Suggested view: Bar chart (avg subscribers), Table (service).
